intro: Dracaenopsis australis, also known as Australian black dracaena, is a plant species within the Asparagaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Dracaenopsis australis include dracaenol and other sesquiterpenes, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or infuse, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
